title: Bridge Essay: Neurocognitive control in dance perception and performance.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Neurocognitive control in dance perception and performance.** — Acta Psychol (Amst). PMID: 22305351. DOI: 10.1016/j.actpsy.2011.12.005. Dance is arich source of material for researchers interested in the integration of movement and cognition. The multiple aspects of embodied cognition involved in performing and perceiving dance have inspired scientists to use dance as ameans for studying motor control, expertise, and action-perception links. The aim of this review is to present basic research on cognitive and neural processes implicated in the execution, expression, and observation of dance, and to bring into relief contempora...
